definitions: To be pursuing a mistaken course of action.
To have a wrong idea or be mistaken about something.
To direct one's efforts or accusations towards the wrong person or thing.
examples: If you think I took your keys, you're barking up the wrong tree; I haven't seen them.
The detective realized he was barking up the wrong tree when the new evidence emerged.
She's barking up the wrong tree if she expects me to help her cheat on the exam.
